messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following online pharmacy privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"PharmaDirect collects your name, address, date of birth, prescription details, and medical conditions to fulfil your medication orders. We process health data on the basis of your explicit consent and as necessary for medical treatment under Art. 9(2)(h). Prescriptions are verified with your registered GP. Data is stored in Ireland and retained for 8 years as required by pharmaceutical regulations. You have the right to access, correct, restrict, port, and erase your data and to object to processing, by contacting privacy@pharmadirect.ie. Our DPO is at dpo@pharmadirect.ie. We use a licensed dispensing software provider under a signed DPA. We notify the DPC within 72 hours of a breach and affected customers without undue delay."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Explicit consent and medical treatment necessity (Art. 6(1)(c) via pharmaceutical regulation) both stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Health data correctly identified. Explicit consent (Art. 9(2)(a)) and medical treatment basis (Art. 9(2)(h)) both cited.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Medication fulfilment and GP verification are clear and specific purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is necessary and proportionate for pharmacy dispensing.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 8-year retention stated and grounded in pharmaceutical regulatory requirements.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) all explicitly listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Dispensing software provider named as processor with signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Ireland-based storage confirmed. No transfer concerns.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@pharmadirect.ie disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour DPC notification and customer notification without undue delay both stated.

VERDICT: Compliant
VIOLATIONS: None
RECOMMENDATIONS: No changes required. Consider referencing the right to lodge a complaint with the DPC (Art. 77) for additional transparency.
